what_it_does: Acceptance test for cc_board reverse_traverse and relations: builds a controlled temporary board fixture, exercises edges-into lookup by opaque target string, kind filtering, fail-loud behavior for unregistered kinds, the unified relations(in/out/both) surface, and an optional hydrate=True round-trip against the real noticeboard if genesis items exist.
observations: Uses a tempfile board with three items (two requests sharing a session, one idea from another session) to create authored_by and promoted_from edges.
reverse_traverse is tested against both session:// and board:// target strings, checking source addresses, kind, target, and embedded item.
kind=None returns every edge into the target; an unregistered kind raises BoardError with an 'unknown edge kind' message.
relations('in') returns only edges_in; relations('out') on a non-board address returns an empty edges_out list plus an explicit edges_out_note.
The real-board hydrate check is gated on the presence of item-e523b30d.md in channel-memory/noticeboard and catches any Exception during hydration.
standouts: Proof-by-use on the real board: reverse_traverse('board://item-e523b30d','promoted_from',hydrate=True) is expected to resolve board://item-15aaf9da through resolve_address.
relations(out) on a non-board address is deliberately empty and annotated, avoiding silent guessing.
Fail-loud validation mirrors the forward traverse behavior for unknown edge kinds.
conventions: Manual PASS/FAIL accumulator with a small ok(name, cond, detail) helper that prints per-test status.
sys.path insertion to import runtime.cc_board as cb.
Controlled fixture mirrors the real genesis chain shape (same session authorship plus promoted_from).
Conditional real-board smoke test skips gracefully when genesis fixtures are absent.
concerns: Global module-level variables (tmp, BD, a, b, c) are created as side effects of importing/running the test file.
The temporary board directory is removed with shutil.rmtree before the real-board section runs, which is fine but makes the file's execution order significant.
The hydrate check catches bare Exception rather than a specific resolver/board error type.
Real-board test relies on the default board_dir inside cc_board without explicit cleanup or isolation.
notes: A hybrid acceptance test that validates reverse graph traversal and the relations API against both a synthetic fixture and the live genesis chain.
questions: What is the exact set of registered edge kinds in cc_board and where is the registry defined?
How does resolve_address handle board:// sources during hydration, and what fields does the resolved record contain?
Is reverse_traverse matching performed on the raw target string, or are addresses normalized before comparison?
What is the intended behavior of relations('both') when the target is neither a board item nor a session?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the stated purpose is to gate H1.2 reverse_traverse + relations behavior, and the file exercises exactly those surfaces.
apparent_themes: Controlled fixture plus real-system smoke verification
intention_signals: The file is trying to certify that reverse graph traversal works by string-matching edge targets, that kind filtering and error handling are consistent with forward traversal, and that the relations surface exposes direction-aware views with explicit empty-state semantics.
novelty: The combination of a deterministic mini-board fixture with an optional live-board hydrate round-trip makes this an acceptance test that bridges synthetic and real data.
contribution: Provides executable assertions and a reusable fixture pattern for reverse_traverse, kind filtering, relations directions, and hydrate behavior on the cc_board surface.
summary_for_embedding: An acceptance test file that validates cc_board reverse_traverse and relations against a controlled temporary fixture and an optional real-board hydrate round-trip.
intention_for_embedding: To gate H1.2 behavior by proving reverse edge traversal, kind filtering, fail-loud validation, and hydration work on both synthetic and live board data.

symbols.does: Test assertion helper that records pass/fail and prints a per-check result line.
symbols.description: The ok helper appends the test name to PASS or FAIL based on a boolean condition, prints an 'ok' or 'FAIL' line, and optionally prints a detail string only on failure. It is the local test harness used by every assertion in the file.
